analysis.semantic_summary: This parser processes F5 BIG-IP web traffic logs, specifically HTTP access logs. It extracts structured data from F5 Networks load balancer logs to monitor web traffic patterns, access attempts, and potential security events.

factors: Uses regex patterns for common fields

optimization_opportunities: Add validation for IP address formats
Implement caching for repeated pattern matches
Add error handling for malformed timestamps
analysis.lua_code:
-- SentinelOne Parser: f5_networks_logs-latest 
-- OCSF Class: Web Traffic (5002)
-- Performance Level: High-volume optimized
-- Generated: 2025-10-13T12:17:56.077954

-- Pre-compile patterns for performance
local patterns = {
    timestamp = "(%d%d%d%d)/(%d%d)/(%d%d)%s+(%d%d):(%d%d):(%d%d)",
    ipv4 = "^(%d+)%.(%d+)%.(%d+)%.(%d+)$",
    kvpair = "([^=]+)=([^,]+)"
}

-- Cached constants
local PRODUCT_METADATA = {
    name = "F5 BIG-IP",
    vendor = "F5 Networks"
}

function transform(record)
    -- Input validation with early return
    if not record or type(record) ~= "table" then
        return nil, "Invalid input record"
    end

    -- Initialize OCSF-compliant output with required fields
    local output = {
        class_uid = 5002,
        class_name = "Web Traffic", 
        category_uid = 4,
        category_name = "Network Activity",
        metadata = {
            product = PRODUCT_METADATA,
            version = "1.0"
        },
        time = os.time() * 1000 -- Default timestamp
    }

    -- Process timestamp if present
    if record.timestamp then
        local year, month, day, hour, min, sec = record.timestamp:match(patterns.timestamp)
        if year then
            -- Convert to UNIX timestamp (milliseconds)
            output.time = os.time({
                year = tonumber(year),
                month = tonumber(month),
                day = tonumber(day),
                hour = tonumber(hour),
                min = tonumber(min),
                sec = tonumber(sec)
            }) * 1000
        end
    end

    -- Extract and validate IP addresses
    if record.client_ip then
        local valid = record.client_ip:match(patterns.ipv4)
        if valid then
            output.src = {ip = record.client_ip}
        end
    end

    if record.server_ip then
        local valid = record.server_ip:match(patterns.ipv4)
        if valid then
            output.dst = {ip = record.server_ip}
        end
    end

    -- Process HTTP specific fields
    if record.http_method then
        output.http = {
            method = record.http_method,
            response = {
                status_code = tonumber(record.status_code) or 0
            }
        }
    end

    -- Extract key-value pairs from message field
    if record.message then
        for k, v in record.message:gmatch(patterns.kvpair) do
            -- Trim whitespace
            k = k:match("^%s*(.-)%s*$")
            v = v:match("^%s*(.-)%s*$")
            
            -- Store extracted values
            if k and v then
                if not output.observables then
                    output.observables = {}
                end
                table.insert(output.observables, {
                    name = k,
                    value = v
                })
            end
        end
    end

    -- Validation of required fields
    if not output.class_uid or output.class_uid == 0 then
        return nil, "Missing required field: class_uid"
    end

    if not output.time then
        return nil, "Invalid timestamp"
    end

    return output
end

-- Error handler wrapper
function safe_transform(record)
    local success, result = pcall(transform, record)
    if not success then
        return nil, string.format("Transform error: %s", result)
    end
    return result
end

test_cases.name: Valid F5 Log Entry
test_cases.expected: Valid OCSF output with all fields mapped
